landing.hero_sub: The platform that connects startups, talent and investors — with AI, matching and real intros.

landing.pricing_sub: Cancel monthly. No hidden fine print.

pricing.headline: Simple pricing. No bullshit.
pricing.subhead: Three tracks — start where you are. Upgrade when you're ready.
pricing.upgrade_note: Every upgrade keeps your profile, your matches and your history.
pricing.faq_cancel_a: Yes — cancel monthly, no minimum term, active instantly.
pricing.faq_stealth_a: Your project is visible anonymously — only category & problem area, no name. Investors can still express interest.
pricing.faq_upgrade_a: One click — your profile carries over fully, you only pay the difference.
pricing.faq_investor_free_a: We want investors on the platform, not kept away from it. Pro pays off for active deal flow.
pricing.faq_intro_a: The intro itself is free — you just need the right tier (Startup or Investor Pro).
pricing.faq_yearly_a: Coming soon — with ~20% off compared to monthly payment.

glossary.tam_simple: The entire market: everyone who could theoretically buy your product if you reached absolutely everyone.
glossary.tam_example: App for home cooks in Germany → all ~20M home cooks.
glossary.tam_impact: Shows investors the maximum opportunity. A big number sounds good, but stay realistic.
glossary.sam_som_simple: SAM = the part you can realistically serve. SOM = what you will actually capture in the next years.
glossary.sam_som_example: Of 20M, 4M use such apps (SAM); in 3 years you reach 100,000 (SOM).
glossary.sam_som_impact: Honest SOM numbers build more trust than a giant TAM.
glossary.usp_simple: The one reason customers pick YOU over the competition.
glossary.usp_example: “The only app that recognizes ingredients from a photo.”
glossary.usp_impact: Without a clear USP your startup looks interchangeable. The sharper, the more convincing.
glossary.traction_simple: Proof people actually want your product: users, revenue, growth, pilot customers.
glossary.traction_impact: The single most important trust factor. Numbers beat promises.
glossary.ask_simple: How much money you are raising and exactly what you will use it for.
glossary.ask_example: “€500,000 for product team & marketing over 18 months.”
glossary.ask_impact: A concrete amount + use of funds shows planning. Vague figures scare investors off.
glossary.moat_simple: What stops copycats – e.g. technology, brand, network or data.
glossary.moat_example: “The more users, the more valuable the community – nobody copies that quickly.”
glossary.moat_impact: Shows your lead is durable, not just a passing trend.
glossary.funding_stage_simple: How far along your startup is – from raw idea to a growing company.
glossary.funding_stage_example: Pre-seed = idea/prototype, Seed = first users, Series A = scaling.
glossary.funding_stage_impact: Determines which investors fit and how much money is typical.
glossary.mrr_simple: Revenue that reliably comes in every month, e.g. from subscriptions.
glossary.mrr_impact: The key metric for subscription models. Growing MRR = healthy growth.
glossary.check_size_simple: How much money an investor typically puts into one startup.
glossary.check_size_example: An angel writes €10k–50k, a VC often €500k+.
glossary.check_size_impact: Helps you find the right investor – checks too big/small do not fit.
glossary.cac_simple: What it costs you to win one new paying customer.
glossary.cac_impact: Must be less than what a customer brings you long-term, or you burn money.
glossary.runway_simple: How long your money lasts before the account is empty.
glossary.runway_impact: Shows how urgently you need revenue or fresh capital.
glossary.equity_simple: Shares in your company. Whoever gets equity owns a piece of it.
glossary.equity_example: A co-founder gets 10% equity instead of salary.
glossary.equity_impact: Giving equity brings help/money but reduces your own share.
